assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9.

The intragroup dividend has created a foreign-currency-denominated monetary receivable between group entities with different functional currencies, and the related foreign exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation so they can affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 includes fair value hedges of recognised assets or liabilities, which is directly relevant once the intragroup dividend has become a recognised receivable.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 also includes cash flow hedges of a recognised asset or liability when foreign-currency cash flow variability could affect profit or loss.
